name: indian-rent-control-drafting
description: Open-source Claude-compatible plugin for drafting Indian rent-control / landlord-tenant litigation pleadings across all major State Rent Control regimes. State-config-aware and case-config-aware design — the structural skeleton of any rent-control pleading is uniform, and the user supplies the applicable State Rent Control Act, the chosen forum (Rent Controller / Court of Small Causes / Rent Authority / Rent Tribunal / Prescribed Authority / Civil Judge as Rent Controller), the ground of eviction relied upon (default in payment of rent / bona-fide personal need / nuisance or damage / unlawful sub-letting without written consent / acquiring alternate suitable accommodation / unauthorised user / structural alteration without consent), the tenancy commencement date, the last paid rent figure, the statutory notice trajectory under Section 106 Transfer of Property Act 1882 read with the applicable State Rent Control Act notice provisions, the standard-rent / fair-rent fixation history (where applicable), and the rent-deposit history (where applicable) via a case-config file and the relevant state-config exemplar. Eleven case-type drafting skills covering eviction on each statutory ground, standard-rent fixation applications, rent-deposit applications, tenancy-termination notices, landlord recovery suits, tenant written statements, revisions / appeals before the Rent Controller / Appellate Authority / High Court, and the Model Tenancy Act 2021 framework for States that have adopted (Andhra Pradesh / Tamil Nadu modified / Uttar Pradesh) or are in the process of adopting. State exemplars for Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999, Delhi Rent Control Act 1958, Tamil Nadu Regulation of Rights and Responsibilities of Landlords and Tenants Act 2017, Karnataka Rent Act 2001, West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act 1997, Uttar Pradesh Urban Buildings (Regulation of Letting, Rent and Eviction) Act 1972, Gujarat Rent Control Act 1947 (as amended), and Andhra Pradesh / Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act 1960. Includes the 6-agent drafting pipeline (Reader -> Format -> Drafter -> Verifier -> Refiner -> Overseer), shared rent-control drafting base with Transfer of Property Act 1882 Sections 105-117 general law of lease, Section 106 termination notice discipline, the doctrine of statutory tenancy and its heritability under Damadilal v. Parashram (1976) 4 SCC 645 and Gian Devi Anand v. Jeevan Kumar (1985) 2 SCC 683, the pagdi system jurisprudence under Section 56 Maharashtra Rent Control Act 1999, the Section 14(1)(e) Delhi Rent Control Act 1958 bona-fide personal need framework with Ragavendra Kumar v. Firm Prem Machinary (2000) 1 SCC 679 and Akhileshwar Kumar v. Mustaqim (2003) 1 SCC 75, the Section 25B Delhi Rent Control Act summary procedure for bona-fide need, the sub-tenancy doctrine under Resham Singh v. Raghbir Singh (1999) 7 SCC 263, the standard-rent / fair-rent fixation methodology, and the state-by-state Rent Controller / Court of Small Causes / Civil Judge / Rent Authority forum-nomenclature taxonomy.
